description: Enhance the medication reminder alert with an interactive confirmation pop-up that asks if the user took the medicine via Yes/No radio buttons. On Yes confirmation, save the intake to history via toggleMedication. On No confirmation, just dismiss the alert.
steps: Add medicationId (numeric) to the ActiveAlert interface so toggleMedication can be called
Replace the simple dismiss button with a pop-up section containing Yes/No radio buttons and a confirm button
On Yes confirmation, call toggleMedication(medicationId) server action to save intake to history, then dismiss the alert
On No confirmation, dismiss the alert without saving
Remove the auto-dismiss timer since user interaction is now required
Ensure responsive Tailwind CSS and semantic HTML are used
acceptance_criteria: Each alert shows radio buttons for Yes and No asking if the user took the medicine
A confirm/submit button is present to submit the selection
Selecting Yes and confirming calls toggleMedication and dismisses the alert
Selecting No and confirming dismisses the alert without saving
The auto-dismiss timer is removed - alerts require user interaction
Responsive Tailwind CSS utility classes are used
Semantic HTML is used for the form elements
The project builds successfully with bun run build
verification: Run 'bun run build' and confirm exit code 0 with no TypeScript errors
findings: Build passes cleanly with bun run build. The component now uses useTransition for the async toggleMedication call to avoid blocking the UI. The auto-dismiss timer was removed since user interaction is required. The medicationId field was added to ActiveAlert to enable calling toggleMedication. Semantic HTML elements (form, fieldset, legend, label) are used for the radio button group. The pre-existing worker_threads warnings remain but do not affect the build.
